req: REQ-032 (Project Need Attachments (NGO reference files for Discovery + build))

issues.detail: The requirement says supported types "include" the named formats; it does not define an exhaustive allow-list or require every other type to be rejected. The unsupported-type assertion and corresponding coverage-map claim add behavior.
issues.fix: Remove the unsupported-type rejection, or test it only after the requirement defines an exhaustive allow-list.

issues.detail: "A Tier-1 project requires no hard acknowledgment" is unqualified and conflicts with REQ-004 and Platform Promise §9, which require a Tier-1 data-responsibility acknowledgment at the applicable funding/match gates.
issues.fix: Scope the assertion narrowly: Tier 1 has no REQ-032-specific fixtures-only upload acknowledgment; do not negate the separate PRD acknowledgment gates.

issues.detail: The new pre-ack rejection has no recovery-path assertion. An implementation that permanently rejects every Tier-2 upload would pass. The clause "Tier-2 adds a hard acknowledgment" requires acknowledgment to enable the governed flow.
issues.fix: After recording the required acknowledgment, retry the same Tier-2 upload and assert that it succeeds and attaches to the project.

issues.detail: "Absence probe" defines no observable oracle. The no-delay/no-block assertion rules out only a blocking scan; an asynchronous post-upload PII or secret scanner could still exist and pass.
issues.fix: Instrument scan-service calls and queued/background jobs, upload a sentinel file, drain post-upload work, and assert zero content-scan invocations or jobs.

issues.detail: Requiring "seeded ignore rules" prescribes an implementation that REQ-032 does not state and that the test itself says is owned elsewhere. A compliant flow downloading outside the repository needs no ignore rule and would fail this test.
issues.fix: Assert only the required outcome: the default download does not enter the repository's normal/tracked contents; leave the mechanism to its owning requirement.

issues.detail: The added multimodal-read test covers only PDF, one image, and CSV. Upload acceptance in AT-032.02 does not prove Discovery can read the named TSV, TXT, DOCX, and XLSX formats, so those supported reference-file paths remain uncovered.
issues.fix: Use content-only sentinels in every named supported format—PDF, image, CSV, TSV, TXT, DOCX, and XLSX—and assert Discovery output derives content from each.
